channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Musk: Trump 'agreed' USAID should be shut down
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said early Monday that President Trump has “agreed” to shut down the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), the agency that administers billions of dollars of humanitarian, development and security assistance in more than 100 countries.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: At least 19 killed mostly women in a car bomb explosion in northern Syria
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — A car bomb exploded on the outskirts of a northern Syrian city on Monday, killing at least 19 people, all but one of them women, and leaving more than a dozen wounded, hospital workers said.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First Trump-Netanyahu meeting: 3 top issues
item.description: President Trump will welcome Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to the White House this week, with tensions between the two leaders standing in stark contrast to the priority both countries place on the bilateral relationship.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Belgium has a new prime minister, one who long tried to gut the nation and seek regional autonomy
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Belgium has a new prime minister whose political purpose was long to break up the nation, gut the state structures and give ever more autonomy to his northern Flanders at the cost of everyone else.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Blast in an upscale residential area in Moscow kills 1 and leaves 4 wounded
item.description: Russian news agencies say a blast in an upscale residential block in Moscow has killed one person and wounded four others.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hospitals in eastern Congo are crowded with wounded and exhausting their supplies
item.description: Hundreds of wounded people have poured into overcrowded hospitals in Goma after Rwanda-backed rebels captured the major city in eastern Congo last week.
item.dc:creator: Vivien Latour, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UK's Starmer seeks strong trade relations with the US in the wake of Trump's tariffs
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday that he would seek a strong trade relationship with the U.S. after President Donald Trump suggested he would slap Europe with tariffs after he hit America's biggest trading partners — Canada, Mexico and China — with import taxes.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Preoccupied with its troops in Russia, North Korea unlikely to embrace Trump's overture soon
item.description: North Korea has lashed out at U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio for calling it a “rogue” state and warned that such remarks will never contribute to U.S. interests.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US-backed commander says his Kurdish-led group wants a secular and civil state in post-Assad Syria
item.description: HASSAKEH, Syria (AP) — The commander of the main U.S.-backed force in Syria said Sunday the recent ouster of the Assad family from power should be followed by building a secular, civil and decentralized state that treats all its citizens equally no matter their religion or ethnicity.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jailed ex-Pakistani PM Imran Khan draws parallels to President Trump in fight for justice
item.description: Former Pakistani Prime Minister Imran Khan looms large over the country's political scene, despite being jailed. Now questions arise as to whether President Trump could bring him some relief.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China to file lawsuit with WTO, vows 'countermeasures' in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: China’s Ministry of Commerce said it would file a legal case against the United States at the World Trade Organization (WTO), in response to President Trump’s decision Saturday to impose 10 percent tariffs on Chinese goods.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: France braces for a political showdown as the new prime minister seeks to force a budget through
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Political uncertainty is again looming in France after Prime Minister Francois Bayrou warned that he would use special executive powers this week to get his budget approved without a vote by lawmakers. The move is expected to trigger a motion of no confidence.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gaza ceasefire sees its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The ceasefire in Gaza saw its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners on Saturday, and the crucial Rafah border crossing reopened two days before discussions on the truce’s far more difficult second phase begin.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and IMAD ISSEID, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Takeaways from the AP interview with Ukraine’s Zelenskyy
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Associated Press interviewed Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Saturday at a crucial juncture of the nearly three-year war against Russia’s full-scale invasion. U.S.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, SAMYA KULLAB and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuela frees 6 Americans after meeting between President Maduro and Trump's envoy
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Six Americans who had been detained in Venezuela in recent months were freed by the government of President Nicolás Maduro after he met Friday with a Trump administration official tasked with urging the authoritarian leader to take back deported migrants who have committed
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he ordered airstrikes on Islamic State in Somalia
item.description: "These killers, who we found hiding in caves, threatened the United States and our Allies," Trump said in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Father of Hamas’ youngest hostages is released
item.description: Yarden Bibas, father of Hamas' youngest hostage, has been released after more than 480 days in captivity, but his wife and two sons remain in Gaza. Their fates are still unknown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio heads to Panama, Latin America to pursue Trump's 'Golden Age' agenda
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is set to visit Panama in his first official visit abroad as part of what the State Department said is to underscore President Donald Trump's vision of re-establishing a "Golden Era.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump uniquely placed to ‘whisper’ in Erdogan’s ear over Turkish regional ambitions: Greek defense minister
item.description: Greek Defense Minister Nikos Dendias said NATO needs to take seriously Turkey's expansionist ambitions and President Erdoğan's desire to "recreate the Ottoman Empire" amid aggression.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump envoy Richard Grenell secures freedom for 6 Americans following meeting with Maduro in Venezuela
item.description: Richard Grenell secured the release of six Americans following a meeting with Venezuela's dictator Nicolas Maduro on Friday. Grenell demanded that Maduro accept deported criminals from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WHO chief asks countries to push Washington to reconsider its withdrawal
item.description: The World Health Organization chief is asking global leaders to lean on Washington to reverse President Donald Trump’s decision to withdraw from the U.N. health agency.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng And Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Investigators recover black boxes from plane in DC crash
item.description: There were 60 passengers and four crew members aboard the plane, which was arriving from Wichita, Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Year of the Snake is underway with festivities around the world
item.description: The holiday — known as the Spring Festival in China, Tet in Vietnam and Seollal in Korea — is a major festival celebrated around the world.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korean leader Kim inspects nuclear facility as Pyongyang pressures Trump administration
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un inspected a facility that produces nuclear material and called for bolstering the country’s nuclear fighting capability, state media reported Wednesday, as the North looks to increase pressure on the United States following the inauguration o
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, NewsNation
